todaysEvents.summary: Iran re-closes Strait of Hormuz (Apr 18-19) after US refuses to lift naval blockade; IRGC strikes vessels attempting to transit; video footage shows ships turning away from strait (CNN, Fortune, CBS, Apr 19-20).
todaysEvents.delta: Escalation: Hormuz reopening reversed — active vessel strikes resume

todaysEvents.summary: Iran formally rejects second Islamabad talks (Apr 19), citing US 'excessive demands' and naval blockade; parliament speaker Qalibaf: 'no retreat in diplomacy'; Trump sends negotiators to Pakistan anyway; ceasefire expires Apr 22 with no deal in sight (IRNA, Time, KPBS, Apr 19).
todaysEvents.delta: Escalation: talks rejected; Apr 22 ceasefire expiry is now 2 days away with no framework

todaysEvents.summary: Brent ~$96 est. (+4.5% on Hormuz re-closure; CNN ~$95.71 Apr 19-20); WTI ~$91.50 est.; iTraxx Asia IG ~190bp est. (+15bp); tail risk rises to 42% as ceasefire expiry clock runs with no agreement (Apr 20).
todaysEvents.delta: Markets reprice sharply higher as Hormuz re-closure reverses April 17 de-escalation rally

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.description: Emergency diplomatic breakthrough before Apr 22: Pakistan back-channel produces last-minute ceasefire extension + partial Hormuz reopening framework. Requires Iran to suspend active vessel interdictions and US to pause blockade enforcement. Brent retraces toward $88-94/bbl; iTraxx Asia IG tightens below 175bp. Very low probability given Iran's formal rejection of Apr 19 talks and active vessel strikes.

scenarios.label: Stress
scenarios.description: Apr 22 ceasefire lapses but IRGC limits escalation to harassment and toll-booth enforcement rather than full closure. Intermittent Hormuz transits continue under elevated war-risk premiums; US and Iran maintain back-channel via Pakistan. Brent stabilises $92-110/bbl; JKM $20-24/MMBtu; iTraxx Asia IG 185-210bp. No formal deal but tacit de-escalation prevents full kinetic resumption.

scenarios.label: Tail
scenarios.description: Apr 22 ceasefire expires without extension; IRGC executes Gulf-wide closure — Hormuz fully blocked, Houthis reactivated in Red Sea, Gulf of Oman interdiction resumed. US-Israel air campaign resumes. Kharg Island seizure or Saudi Aramco strike brings GCC production under acute threat. Brent spikes $130-170/bbl; LNG markets seize; ASEAN recession risk acute. Gold surges past $5,000/oz.
